hot take i really like the design of the iphone pros not because its new well maybe partially but rather because its industrial and utilitarianfunction over form finally it looks and feels less like jewelry and more like a professional media tool which is what it shouldve been ever since it was called pro aluminum may be easy to scratch and dent and is much less premium than titanium but together with the vapor chamber it significantly helps with thermals resulting in less overheating and throttling especially when shooting photos and videos under the philippine sun still salty that theres no black though id be so annoyed if apple decides to release one midcycle haha but you know what this silver is really growing on me especially because its so easy to find cases to go well with it haha and yes just like the previous years i still chose the smaller pro as my main device why i do and you should probably too i plan to share in a future video but despite the iphones being the main characters its the airpods pro that i was excited about the most whether it sounds better is up for debate imo the app sounds more wellrounded but its active noise cancellation is definitely better funny story i thought the unit that i got was defective because of the whistling sound the right airpod was making turns out my right ear was just congested the whistling stopped after i had my ears professionally declogged by an ent haha now i have to admit i got the apple watch series mostly because its part of the apple trio iphone airpods watch traditionally our iphone unboxing videos always include all three of them and so for the first time we got the base noncellular variant this year it wont replace my titanium series and im most probably gonna sell it after well see timepieces are fashion accessories as well so i tend to upgrade only when the new one has a different and presumably betterlooking design anyway thank you guys for watching but this is not our last iphone unboxing this year far from it stay tuned for the next one

comment_clean: usb or wifi note that wifi is wireless ethernet ergo you could also use an ethernet cat cat etc cable assuming your printer has an ethernet port if you use hps instant ink program note that it will not work with a usb connection to use instant ink you must connect your hp printer to your cable modem router your internet gateway which allows your hp printer to send ink level values and page volume numbers to hps servers and also allows hp to send your printer firmware updates it will also allow you if you set it up to send printouts to your printer from any internet connected device on the planet that means that either a wifi connection or an ethernet cable connection is necessary not usb also note that usb cables will generally have to be no longer than meters long and that is flirting with usbs functional limit wifi distance reliability will vary depending on the protocol and how many walls you must pass the signal though and the materials in those walls and competing wifi interference from neighbors with an ethernet cable any cat x version you will be in good shape for meters no signal issues you will always have a solid connection and your printer can be kept off of anyone elses radar who is sniffing for wireless signals if you want to be able to print from your laptop in your back yard then wifi is your solution but if you can run an ethernet cable then that will be your most secure most reliable and fastest connection to your printer
